notes: Researched and looked for all possible ways we could utilize the useSchema() React hook from Sanity Studio which so far is the only method to generate the full schema registry or the schemas that are currently defined for a sanity project. I checked with their HTTP API documentation, also tried running both the project and query endpoints and asking the AI tool from their Slack channel to confirm if there's an existing way that works closely like useSchema, to no success. I created an Eraser dashboard (notes + canvas for diagrams) where Dorell and I could add our ideas, challenges and other relevant information to come up with a solution to this. This is the draft so far: https://app.eraser.io/workspace/ReOtCu1Ma7KlE0lUPr7F?origin=share

module_detail.name: StackShift AI Scraper | Add components to section patterns used for prompting

notes:
Checked the current project files to identify where else to define static schema data useful to improve the prompts processed by JINA.AI. With the latest schema registry data, extracted all the fields and created a separate JSON file for it. Updated the supabase function "analyze-sections" with the schema registry SECTION data types as supported in StackShift default and the new data for the FIELDS to use as another source of truth for the generated JSON data when processing URLs. Committed changes to the main branch - 
https://github.com/webriq/ai-scraper-next/commit/9c9d859d5082c346d9df4bf22e41b910dea6976d, then processed another test URL on my account but the processed data is still either incomplete or incorrect.

module_detail.name: StackShift AI Scraper | Fix Sanity sections list

notes: [Local development environment] Updated the all the pre-defined schema JSON files for the components we support by default in StackShift studio to follow the object format: { "schema-type": { "label": "short description of the schema type", "variants": { // contains sub-objects of fields per variant of this schema type } } }. This is so to improve the displayed sections list when a sanity project is connected from the processed URL and also to possibly generate better component suggestion results using the AI prompt. As of this writing, the AI prompt results are still inaccurate with the actual components and their variants that we support.

module_detail.name: AI Scraper - Update the analyze suggestion prompt for sections

notes: Tried to update the current analyze-suggestions endpoint prompt to use the saved analysis data and improve each results with 'retry analysis' by letting it read if the previous suggested component has the following keys: skipComponent (remove component in next iteration), notes (apply the corrections for type or variant or content mapping on the added component notes), suggestedVariant vs variant where if a 'variant' key is found and is different from the 'suggestedVariant' this means that the 'suggestedVariant' was incorrect and succeeding iterations should follow the 'variant' value (e.g. suggestedVariant = variant_d and variant = variant_a) and originalType vs type where if a 'type' key is found and is different from the 'originalType' this means that the 'originalType' was incorrect and succeeding iterations should follow the 'variant' value (e.g. originalType = features and type = header). I want to test this approach if it helps improve every iteration returned by the scraper content analysis with the component fields mapped. However, current iterations still do not return better results.
log_notes: Tried to update the current analyze-suggestions endpoint prompt to use the saved analysis data and improve each results with 'retry analysis' by letting it read if the previous suggested component has the following keys: skipComponent (remove component in next iteration), notes (apply the corrections for type or variant or content mapping on the added component notes), suggestedVariant vs variant where if a 'variant' key is found and is different from the 'suggestedVariant' this means that the 'suggestedVariant' was incorrect and succeeding iterations should follow the 'variant' value (e.g. suggestedVariant = variant_d and variant = variant_a) and originalType vs type where if a 'type' key is found and is different from the 'originalType' this means that the 'originalType' was incorrect and succeeding iterations should follow the 'variant' value (e.g. originalType = features and type = header). I want to test this approach if it helps improve every iteration returned by the scraper content analysis with the component fields mapped. However, current iterations still do not return better results.

module_detail.name: Research and Discovery on AI and LLM tools

notes:
After my initial testing with Claude Desktop + Sanity MCP, shared with the team one of the outputs I got from one of the prompts I did: 
https://claude.ai/share/b08ab400-2e24-4c59-94dc-495c35d4721c. The MCP tools such as "get_initial_context" can get the whole schema of the studio project and return detailed info of the fields including their types. It also has GROQ query and get document ID tools which are useful to find existing references, documents and images and later, identify the suitable page type to create based on the analyzed content. It can also be used to create the page (tho we might not need this since we already have APIs setup to handle this). I also did some research on how we could integrate the Sanity MCP Server to the current project. There isn't much documentation about this yet but we can cross-check with the available HTTP API calls from Sanity Studio: https://www.sanity.io/docs/js-client. I have updated the notes I've gathered up here as well: https://app.eraser.io/workspace/vH0OC26etQfHWOeEAuep?origin=share

notes:
Testing done with the AI Scraper using the current live/production URL on the same website Seva Dental: 
https://ai-scraper.webriq.com/project/6814d05a-f33f-40c0-bd8a-74b034af0073 linked to test project stackshift-sevadentaltestapril7-bdhy. The URL processing into markdown then JSON to analyze StackShift components have improved since. For the home page alone we get the navigation , some of the common components correctly point to 'header' (versus always features before) tho some still needed changing of the type but the common data is now correctly mapped in between these changes. The pages created to the connected studio also get the same data from the scraper: https://stackshift-sevadentaltestapril7-bdhy.webriq.us/studio/desk/pages;Fa8h1HN3DlhXEocYFLEh7c (preview: https://stackshift-sevadentaltestapril7-bdhy.webriq.us/be-our-guest), https://stackshift-sevadentaltestapril7-bdhy.webriq.us/studio/desk/pages;liBwLfU12KkZimf6bMulaD (preview: https://stackshift-sevadentaltestapril7-bdhy.webriq.us/). Noted fixes needed so far: the processing from Markdown to JSON where not all data are correctly processed into JSON so they don't get included when analyzing components; some images are not accessible from the website so they cannot be used for image processing (not uploaded to the target studio if doesn't exists)
log_notes:
Testing done with the AI Scraper using the current live/production URL on the same website Seva Dental: 
https://ai-scraper.webriq.com/project/6814d05a-f33f-40c0-bd8a-74b034af0073 linked to test project stackshift-sevadentaltestapril7-bdhy. The URL processing into markdown then JSON to analyze StackShift components have improved since. For the home page alone we get the navigation , some of the common components correctly point to 'header' (versus always features before) tho some still needed changing of the type but the common data is now correctly mapped in between these changes. The pages created to the connected studio also get the same data from the scraper: https://stackshift-sevadentaltestapril7-bdhy.webriq.us/studio/desk/pages;Fa8h1HN3DlhXEocYFLEh7c (preview: https://stackshift-sevadentaltestapril7-bdhy.webriq.us/be-our-guest), https://stackshift-sevadentaltestapril7-bdhy.webriq.us/studio/desk/pages;liBwLfU12KkZimf6bMulaD (preview: https://stackshift-sevadentaltestapril7-bdhy.webriq.us/). Noted fixes needed so far: the processing from Markdown to JSON where not all data are correctly processed into JSON so they don't get included when analyzing components; some images are not accessible from the website so they cannot be used for image processing (not uploaded to the target studio if doesn't exists)

module_detail.name: AI Scraper - Update scrape module to use Playwright

notes: Worked on adding the initial code revisions for the API and edge function to implement the scrape module to use Playwright (replace Jina AI) from a dev branch "feat/playwright". On my local environment, I have added the file changes: (1) /api/get-markdown (API, new) - use playwright for web scraping and finally return the markdown and metadata; (2) /api/process (API, existing) - replace the edge function 'scrape-url' call that uses Jina AI with the 'get-markdown' API call and move batch requests for 'analyze-sections' edge function; (3) /supabase/functions/section-analysis (Supabase function, new) - process screenshot and markdown from Supabase and divide content into sections; and (4) /api/analyze-suggestions (API, existing) - simplify current logic flow using the 'section-analysis' edge function data to process into Sanity structured schema. I will commit these changes later after I test the production AI Scraper with Quandary project. (Update 8:19pm commit history of changes mentioned above: https://github.com/webriq/ai-scraper-next/commit/9d3792d2aa047f48d268978ff3e3f57bdcfa574b)

module_detail.name: AI Scraper - Update scrape module edge function to use Astral

notes: I tested out the Playwright/Puppeteer alternative that works in Deno named Astral (https://github.com/lino-levan/astral) after adding commits: https://github.com/webriq/ai-scraper-next/commit/a5d66e94d74c1621dfc89cbebfbca5130b54df7d and https://github.com/webriq/ai-scraper-next/commit/f164cdbb351ca415d26b0e22951c1273af41e5a1; where I called it from the scrape-url Supabase Edge function directly since it has a Deno library. However after testing it from preview URL https://ai-scraper-next-git-feat-playwright-webriqhq.vercel.app/ it still doesn't work with Deno Deploy (https://github.com/lino-levan/astral/issues/140). This is one of its current limitations for environments like Supabase Edge functions they require external APIs. It also can't be done via Vercel serverless functions because Astral only has a Deno library - it isn't packaged in NPM yet.

module_detail.name: AI Scraper - Implement working scrape module with Puppeteer

notes:
While the Coolify setup with puppeteer is being resolved, I verified our current prompts on the OpenAI playground to confirm tokens used and processing time.
For the WebriQ website home page alone: (1) API#1 (markdown to JSON) = 21.2k tokens (190s); (2) API#2 (JSON to StackShift data) = 18.576k tokens (118s) So general estimation (based on current data): ≈20 URLs (at 2 API requests each) = 1M tokens. I also checked: Vision fine-tuning https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/vision-fine-tuning; and Reinforcement fine-tuning (RFT) https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/reinforcement-fine-tuning?grader-switcher=grader_json documentations to see if we could make use of this to refine our results. I thought about training the model with section image patterns to improve recognition, but since we'll be implementing custom sanity MCP soon and the data we process isn't repetitive (dynamic data, different per website) I still need to look into more if this is needed. I have sent my findings and ideas to the team and have also asked their opinions.

On first run, I was able to get the worker to run completely: https://dash.cloudflare.com/7760b0a957a11c1c580929f96ee0b2c2/workers/services/view/browser-worker/production/observability/logs?view=invocations&time=%7B%22value%22%3A5%2C%22unit%22%3A%22minutes%22%2C%22type%22%3A%22relative%22%7D, including passing the data to the web service: https://supabase.com/dashboard/project/rlmyyetnvghtjcbzaktd/functions/webhook-receiver/logs?its=2025-05-08T15:30:26.687Z&ite=. However, I need to fix the caller edge function because it got an error response from the worker function. After adding the fixes https://github.com/webriq/puppeteer-cf-worker/commit/982c252a578bff164ea8b03bb410093a754ee479 and even implementing the reusing sessions to resolve the browser timeout https://github.com/webriq/puppeteer-cf-worker/commit/868a546e519393aaf3df62500b6364df5261650c, I still can't get it to work.

module_detail.name: AI Scraper - Update OpenAI model and prompts

notes: Additional testing done with the current setup after the previous commits but still didn't get at least 80% of the markdown conversion. So, adjusted the prompt but went back to use gpt-4.1-mini model which is less expensive than o4-mini but is more intelligent and has a larger context than gpt-4o-mini (https://platform.openai.com/docs/models/compare?model=gpt-4.1-mini) at a $1 difference per 1M tokens. module_detail.name: AI Scraper - Fix puppeteer deploy in Coolify to handle multiple URL requests

notes: Investigate the issue with the Coolify deploy with puppeteer -- I have tried updating the code, redeploying and testing new instances 1 URL at a time from the scraper however the issue still persists. I also did some research on how we could possibly implement this to handle multiple requests, I even came across this possible setting for the dockerfile in Coolify maybe its the reason why the puppeteer browser instances aren't closed gracefully https://pptr.dev/troubleshooting#running-puppeteer-in-docker and the possibility of implementing the queueing here. Added commits to the repo - https://github.com/webriq/coolify-puppeteer-api/commit/ced4f71189ec17c130ee223b43bd2124d8d5a2bc, https://github.com/webriq/coolify-puppeteer-api/commit/fcb4fdd492fb334f81aad347a2470cace57db615 and https://github.com/webriq/coolify-puppeteer-api/commit/a08b105d479754fcf687ef058382b3d443447c45

notes: Investigated the blank page issue flagged by the team working on the scraped pages for drpoz.com. Upon checking, all the website content (left pane, analysis tab) was scraped successfully, what went wrong is the components processing (right pane - "component suggestions"). I believe the page type was not set correctly before the page was created hence the expected content wasn't structured for the target page that was created in the studio. It wasn't a bug, but user error. It's the same situation for these scraped pages as well: https://drpoz.com/author/drproz/ , https://drpoz.com/blog/, https://drpoz.com/category/uncategorized/, https://drpoz.com/category/pediatric-dentistry/, https://drpoz.com/category/general/, https://drpoz.com/author/dickkaverman/

notes: Investigate an issue reported by April while using the AI Scraper for site https://mikuladental.com where ALL processed URLs returned a 403 response including the screenshot. I checked with the backend and the web scraper (puppeteer-cluster service deployed in Coolify) did not return any error and the responses were streamed to the 2 edge functions successfully. However upon closer look with the logs, I was able to notice that no HTML data was processed and that the screenshot images only showed the 403 response. It seems that the website has blocked the scraper and couldn't proceed with getting its data. I tried to check again if we could implement something like the puppeteer-extra-plugin-stealth package (https://www.npmjs.com/package/puppeteer-extra-plugin-stealth) together on top with the existing puppeteer-cluster (https://www.npmjs.com/package/puppeteer-cluster) to bypass blockers or maybe anonymize user agents. This requires code changes and further verification if implemented and is an isolated case. Taking note of the URLs we're unable to process for now to that reason and ask April to process another domain to proceed with the exercise.

module_detail.name: ContentForge backend service | Update Supabase tables

notes: Worked on revising the logic flow of the Supabase tables and functions. Previously, the logic flow of saving and retrieving data was to query by chat session (ID). It should be to query data by project (ID) instead, where every project has a knowledge base and is populated from every chat session. So new chats should get info from the populated knowledge base, thus the table relationships and function should be updated. Updated the Supabase tables https://supabase.com/dashboard/project/mzcknyrulewrnyqdnddo/database/tables and defined an updated function to search the knowledge base to include the project_id https://supabase.com/dashboard/project/mzcknyrulewrnyqdnddo/database/functions?search=search_knowledge_base_updated.

module_detail.name: PublishForge | Revise chat architecture flow - Enhance implementation

notes: Worked on added enhancements for the chat workflow to: (1) Add persistent context system with database storage, shared memory and error tracking; (2) Add caching strategy for cached queries to improve KB performance; (3) Improve file processing (PDF, CSV, TXT, JSON, Markdown) with smart chunking and deduplication; and (4) Add standardized response format for consistency across all endpoints - https://github.com/webriq/publishforge/commit/b64a8522ace03d5035ccd7bcff14148919f1a787 and fixes to the chat on found issues after testing on previous commit - https://github.com/webriq/publishforge/commit/64e8e237abcd48ac2ce2228f79ec2ecab1f44414
log_notes: Worked on added enhancements for the chat workflow to: (1) Add persistent context system with database storage, shared memory and error tracking; (2) Add caching strategy for cached queries to improve KB performance; (3) Improve file processing (PDF, CSV, TXT, JSON, Markdown) with smart chunking and deduplication; and (4) Add standardized response format for consistency across all endpoints - https://github.com/webriq/publishforge/commit/b64a8522ace03d5035ccd7bcff14148919f1a787 and fixes to the chat on found issues after testing on previous commit - https://github.com/webriq/publishforge/commit/64e8e237abcd48ac2ce2228f79ec2ecab1f44414

module_detail.name: PublishForge | Revise chat architecture flow - Enhance implementation

notes: Currently working on resolving the issue where chat context is only saved from the current chat session device which means leaving the current session doesn't continue the chat where user left off. It also affects the content generation and general listing responses returned by the LLM since the context was not retained. Might have to redesign the RAG workflow and agent-tool orchestration to make this work again.
